pick.rationale: Louisville enters this 6/11 matchup with a significant statistical advantage, ranking inside the top 20 in both KenPom and Torvik compared to South Florida's standing at 50th. The Cardinals boast an elite offense that ranks in the top 25 nationally, though their ceiling may be impacted by the questionable status of star point guard Mikel Brown Jr. due to a lingering back injury. While the Bulls are a capable and balanced squad that could capitalize if Louisville is shorthanded, the Cardinals' overall talent depth and superior defensive efficiency should provide enough of a cushion to survive a potential upset bid and advance.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze Louisville vs South Florida Matchup and Injury Context
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated the statistical profiles of both teams and investigated the injury status of Louisville's Mikel Brown Jr.
evidence: Louisville ranks 18th in KenPom and 16th in Torvik, featuring a top-25 adjusted offense.
South Florida ranks 50th in both KenPom and Torvik, presenting a balanced but less efficient profile.
Louisville star point guard Mikel Brown Jr. is questionable with a back injury; the team went 7-5 in games he missed this season.
Despite the injury concern, Louisville's nearly 10-point advantage in adjusted efficiency margin makes them the favorite.

Brown Louisville Courier Journal Show Caption Hide Caption Louisville basketball's Mikel Brown Jr. provides back injury update Mikel Brown Jr. breaks down the back injury keeping him out of the ACC Tournament and gives an update on his recovery for the NCAA Tournament. Louisville basketball received a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament on Selection Sunday. The Cardinals rated well in many key metrics, but they went just 7-5 in games Mikel Brown Jr. missed due to injury. UofL will need its star point guard healthy to navigate its challenging path for a deep March Madness run. Louisville basketball , in a season of many what-ifs, just had another on Selection Sunday with the NCAA’s release of the tournament bracket . The Cardinals received a No. 6 seed in the East Region and will face No. 11-seed and American Conference champion South Florida on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. in the first round. That’s at least one seed line higher than they should be. As much as the fate of how long they last in the nation’s best championship tournament will be determined by the availability of star freshman guard Mikel Brown Jr. , their position as a 6-seed can be attributed to him too. Through no fault of his own, the back injury that forced Brown to miss 10 regular-season games and kept him sidelined for the entire ACC

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
